EXISTS utterances (\n52\t    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n53\t    session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n54\t    raw_text    TEXT NOT NULL,\n55\t    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n56\t    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n57\t);\n58\t\n59\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_utterances_session ON utterances(session_id);\n60\t\n61\t-- Intents: parsed intent threads from utterances\n62\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS intents (\n63\t    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n64\t    utterance_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n65\t    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n66\t    topic_id     TEXT,\n67\t    project_slug TEXT,\n68\t    intent_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n69\t    lookup_kind  TEXT,  -- lookup intents only: 'logs' | 'config' | 'docs' (router-emitted; see Intent Router). The hot-path\n97\t                       -- component lookup keys on this column, no LLM (see UI-Regen Agent /\n98\t                       -- component_usage_patterns).\n99\t    card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK(card_fallback IN (0, 1)),  -- 1 when no component matched this result_type (or below threshold): the client renders the built-in generic fallback card (see Component Library → Built-in generic fallback card). 0 when a real component rendered it (card_cache holds the rendered HTML).\n100\t    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n101\t    surfaced_at INTEGER,\n102\t    acked_at    INTEGER,\n103\t    previous_result_id TEXT,  -- Link to previous result for diff\n104\t    diff_summary TEXT,  -- Human-readable diff summary\n105\t    diff_data    TEXT,  -- JSON: detailed field diffs\n106\t    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n107\t    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n108\t    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n109\t    FOREIGN KEY (previous_result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n110\t);\n111\t\n112\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_session ON results(session_id);\n113\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_topic ON results(topic_id);\n114\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_created ON results(created_at);\n115\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_previous ON results(previous_result_id);\n116\t\n117\t-- Topics: persistent concerns organizing intents and results\n118\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS topics (\n119\t    id project_slugs TEXT,  -- JSON array\n123\t    scope        TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(scope IN ('session', 'cross-session', 'global')) DEFAULT 'session',\n124\t    session_id   TEXT,\n125\t    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n126\t    last_active  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n127\t    archived_at  INTEGER,\n128\t    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n129\t);\n130\t\n131\tCREATE behavior tracking for background analysis\n156\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS feedback_signals (\n157\t    signal_id    TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n158\t    signal_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n159\t    session_id   TEXT TEXT,\n165\t    processed    INTEGER DEFAULT 0 CHECK(processed IN (0, 1)),\n166\t    processed_at INTEGER,\n167\t    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n168\t idx_signals_session ON feedback_signals(session_id);\n173\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_type ON feedback_signals(signal_type);\n174\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_processed ON feedback_signals(processed);\n175\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_result ON feedback_signals(result_id);\n176\t\n177\t-- Per-stage latency capture for every dispatch (Latency Budget & Instrumentation).\n178\t-- Keyed by the intent *thread* id (routed_intent.intent_id — the same id the\n179\t-- fetch/synthesize/escalate strands and the results row use), NOT the\n180\t-- intents.id the caller mints in create_intent(). router_ms is shared across\n181\t-- every intent thread from the same utterance; escalate_ms is NULL for hot-path\n182\t-- dispatches; stt_ms/first_render_ms are client-reported and NULL when the\n183\t-- client never reports them. synthesize_first_token_ms is NULL until the\n184\t-- synthesize strand streams (the non-streaming call_simple path can't measure\n185\t-- first token separately) — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n186\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dispatch_timings (\n187\t    intent_id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n188\t    router_ms                 INTEGER,\n189\t    fetch_first_source_ms     INTEGER,\n190\t    fetch_total_ms            INTEGER,\n191\t    synthesize_first_token_ms INTEGER,\n192\t    synthesize_total_ms       INTEGER,\n193\t    escalate_ms               INTEGER,\n194\t    sse_emit_ms               INTEGER,\n195\t    stt_ms                    INTEGER,\n196\t    first_render_ms           INTEGER,\n197\t    created_at                INTEGER NOT NULL\n198\t);\n199\t\n200\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dispatch_timings_created ON dispatch_timings(created_at);\n201\t\n202\t-- Bead watch table: circuit breaker tracking for async beads\n203\t-- Persistence layer for the async-path circuit breaker (plan §10 The Async Path).\n204\t-- Tracks open beads watched by the BeadWatcher, recording refusal counts,\n205\t-- last refusal reasons, SLA deadlines, and fencing state. All state is persisted\n206\t-- so a watcher restart loses nothing -- breaker state lives in the database,\n207\t-- not only in memory.\n208\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bead_watch (\n209\t    bead_ref           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,  -- References bead ID (intents.bead_ref)\n210\t    refusal_count      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of REFUSED: comments seen\n211\t    last_refusal_reason TEXT,  -- Most recent refusal reason\n212\t    last_refusal_at    INTEGER,  -- Timestamp of most recent refusal\n213\t    comment_high_water INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT -1,  -- Latest comment index processed (-1 = none)\n214\t    sla_deadline       INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- Unix timestamp when SLA expires\n215\t    sla_flagged_at     INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when SLA was flagged (NULL if not flagged)\n216\t    fenced_at          INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when bead was fenced to status=blocked (NULL if not fenced)\n217\t    created_at         INTEGER NOT NULL  -- When this watch row was created\n218\t);\n219\t\n220\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline ON bead_watch(sla_deadline);\n221\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_watch_fenced ON bead_watch(fenced_at);\n222\t\"\"\"\n223\t\n224\t# The set of dispatch_timings timing columns record_dispatch_timings() may set.\n225\t# intent_id/created_at are handled explicitly (PK / insert-only timestamp).\n226\tDISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS = (\n227\t    \"router_ms\",\n228\t    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n229\t    \"fetch_total_ms\",\n230\t    \"synthesize_first_token_ms\",\n231\t    \"synthesize_total_ms\",\n232\t    \"escalate_ms\",\n233\t    \"sse_emit_ms\",\n234\t    \"stt_ms\",\n235\t    \"first_render_ms\",\n236\t)\n237\t\n238\t# Valid intent status values (plan §10 The Async Path: stuck/failed added for circuit breaker)\n239\tINTENT_STATUSES = ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')\n240\t\n241\t# Default SLA hours per intent type (plan §10 The Async Path: Visible Aging)\n242\t# task-profile: 6h (async bead-backed tasks)\n243\t# hot-path intents: 30s (budget is 3s, flag at 10x for aged pending)\n244\tDEFAULT_SLA_HOURS: dict[str, float] = {\n245\t    \"task-profile\": 6.0,  # 6 hours for async bead-backed tasks\n246\t    \"status\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path status intents\n247\t    \"action\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path action intents\n248\t    \"lookup\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path lookup intents\n249\t    \"brainstorm\": 0.5,   # 30 minutes for brainstorm (may need user iteration)\n250\t    \"reminder\": 24.0,    # 24 hours for reminders\n251\t}\n252\t\n253\t# Circuit breaker thresholds (plan §10 The Async Path)\n254\tCIRCUIT_BREAKER_REFUSAL_THRESHOLD = 3  # Fence after N refusals\n255\tCIRCUIT_BREAKER_AGE_THRESHOLD_HOURS = 24.0  # Fence after N hours without progress\n256\t\n257\t\n258\tdef derive_result_type(\n259\t    intent_type: Optional[str],\n260\t    project_slug: Optional[str],\n261\t    lookup_kind: Optional[str] = None,\n262\t) -> str:\n263\t    \"\"\"Derive the deterministic card-selector key written to ``results.result_type``.\n264\t\n265\t    - intent-derived: ``\"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\"`` — one per intent thread\n266\t      (the aggregated thread card), never per fetch source.\n267\t    - lookup threads insert the router's ``lookup_kind``:\n268\t      ``\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\"`` (e.g. ``lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp`` vs\n269\t      ``lookup:config:ibkr-mcp`` — distinct keys, distinct cards).\n270\t    - monitoring-originated rows: ``\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"``.\n271\t\n272\t    A missing project slug collapses to ``\"general\"`` so the key is always a\n273\t    non-empty, colon-segmented string the selector can index on.\n274\t    \"\"\"\n275\t    slug = project_slug or \"general\"\n276\t    itype = intent_type or \"status\"\n277\t    if itype == \"monitoring\":\n278\t        return f\"monitoring:{slug}\"\n279\t    if itype == \"lookup\" and lookup_kind:\n280\t        return f\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{slug}\"\n281\t    return f\"{itype}:{slug}\"\n282\t\n283\t\n284\tclass SessionStore:\n285\t    \"\"\"Session store with WAL mode for concurrent access.\"\"\"\n286\t\n287\t    def __init__(self, db_path: Path):\n288\t        self.db_path = db_path\n289\t\n290\t    async def initialize(self) -> None:\n291\t        \"\"\"Initialize database with schema and WAL mode.\"\"\"\n292\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n293\t            # Enable WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes\n294\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n295\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n296\t\n297\t            # Create schema\n298\t            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n299\t            await db.commit()\n300\t\n301\t            # Additive migrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS above creates new\n302\t            # columns only for freshly-made DBs; an existing data/session.db\n303\t            # (e.g. the live Hetzner box) keeps its old column set and must be\n304\t            # ALTERed. Each statement is guarded by a PRAGMA table_info probe so\n305\t            # it is idempotent and never re-runs once the column exists.\n306\t            await self._migrate_additive_columns(db)\n307\t\n308\t    @staticmethod\n309\t    async def _migrate_additive_columns(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n310\t        \"\"\"Idempotently add columns introduced after the initial schema.\"\"\"\n311\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(results)\") as cur:\n312\t            result_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n313\t        if \"result_type\" not in result_cols:\n314\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN result_type TEXT\")\n315\t        if \"card_fallback\" not in result_cols:\n316\t            await db.execute(\n317\t                \"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\"\n318\t            )\n319\t\n320\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n321\t            intent_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n322\t        if \"lookup_kind\" not in intent_cols:\n323\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents ADD COLUMN lookup_kind TEXT\")\n324\t\n325\t        # Migrate intents.status CHECK constraint to include 'stuck' and 'failed'\n326\t        # SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we need to recreate the table\n327\t        # First, check if the table still has the old constraint (without stuck/failed)\n328\t        await SessionStore._migrate_intents_status_enum(db)\n329\t\n330\t        # Migrate results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\n331\t        await SessionStore._migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db)\n332\t\n333\t        # Migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1\n334\t        await SessionStore._migrate_bead_watch_comment_high_water(db)\n335\t\n336\t        await db.commit()\n337\t\n338\t    @staticmethod\n339\t    async def _migrate_intents_status_enum(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n340\t        \"\"\"Migrate intents table to support 'stuck' and 'failed' status values.\n341\t\n342\t        Plan §10 The Async Path: extend intents status enum with stuck/failed.\n343\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we recreate the table.\n344\t        \"\"\"\n345\t        # Check if migration is needed by looking for 'stuck' in the schema\n346\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n347\t            cols = await cur.fetchall()\n348\t            status_col = None\n349\t            for col in cols:\n350\t                if col[1] == \"status\":\n351\t                    status_col = col\n352\t                    break\n353\t\n354\t        # If no status column or already has stuck/failed, skip migration\n355\t        if not status_col:\n356\t            return\n357\t\n358\t        # Check if the CHECK constraint already includes stuck/failed\n359\t        # by inspecting the SQL for the intents table\n360\t        async with db.execute(\n361\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='intents'\"\n362\t        ) as cur:\n363\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n364\t            if table_sql and (\"'stuck'\" in table_sql[0] or \"'failed'\" in table_sql[0]):\n365\t                # Already migrated\n366\t                return\n367\t\n368\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new constraint\n369\t        logger.info(\"Migrating intents.status to include 'stuck' and 'failed' values\")\n370\t\n371\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n372\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n373\t\n374\t        try:\n375\t            # Create new table with updated constraint\n376\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n377\t                CREATE TABLE intents_new (\n378\t                    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n379\t                    utterance_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n380\t                    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n381\t                    topic_id     TEXT,\n382\t                    project_slug TEXT,\n383\t                    intent_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n384\t                    lookup_kind  TEXT,\n385\t                    status       TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')) DEFAULT 'pending',\n386\t                    bead_ref     TEXT,\n387\t                    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n388\t                    resolved_at  INTEGER,\n389\t                    FOREIGN KEY (utterance_id) REFERENCES utterances(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n390\t                    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n391\t                    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n392\t                )\n393\t            \"\"\")\n394\t\n395\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n396\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n397\t                INSERT INTO intents_new\n398\t                SELECT id, utterance_id, session_id, topic_id, project_slug,\n399\t                       intent_type, lookup_kind, status, bead_ref, created_at, resolved_at\n400\t                FROM intents\n401\t            \"\"\")\n402\t\n403\t            # Recreate indexes\n404\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_session\")\n405\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_topic\")\n406\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_status\")\n407\t\n408\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_session ON intents_new(session_id)\")\n409\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_topic ON intents_new(topic_id)\")\n410\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_status ON intents_new(status)\")\n411\t\n412\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n413\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE intents\")\n414\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents_new RENAME TO intents\")\n415\t\n416\t            await db.commit()\n417\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: intents.status now supports 'stuck' and 'failed'\")\n418\t\n419\t        except Exception as e:\n420\t            await db.rollback()\n421\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate intents.status: {e}\")\n422\t            raise\n423\t\n424\t    @staticmethod\n425\t    async def _migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n426\t        \"\"\"Migrate results table to allow NULL intent_id for monitoring-originated results.\n427\t\n428\t        Plan §10 Bead Watcher: monitoring-originated results have intent_id=NULL.\n429\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN to drop NOT NULL, so we recreate the table.\n430\t        \"\"\"\n431\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the results table schema\n432\t        async with db.execute(\n433\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='results'\"\n434\t        ) as cur:\n435\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n436\t            if not table_sql:\n437\t                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n438\t\n439\t            # If intent_id is already nullable (NOT NULL not present), skip migration\n440\t            if \"intent_id   TEXT,\" in table_sql[0] or \"intent_id TEXT,\" in table_sql[0]:\n441\t                if \"NOT NULL\" not in table_sql[0].split(\"intent_id\")[1].split(\",\")[0]:\n442\t                    # Already migrated\n443\t                    return\n444\t\n445\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with nullable intent_id\n446\t        logger.info(\"Migrating results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\")\n447\t\n448\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n449\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n450\t\n451\t        try:\n452\t            # Create new table with nullable intent_id\n453\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n454\t                CREATE TABLE results_new (\n455\t                    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n456\t                    intent_id   TEXT,\n457\t                    topic_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\n458\t                    session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n459\t                    summary     TEXT NOT NULL,\n460\t                    data        TEXT NOT NULL,\n461\t                    urgency     TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(urgency IN ('critical', 'high', 'normal', 'low')) DEFAULT 'normal',\n462\t                    result_type TEXT,\n463\t                    card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK(card_fallback IN (0, 1)),\n464\t                    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n465\t                    surfaced_at INTEGER,\n466\t                    acked_at    INTEGER,\n467\t                    previous_result_id TEXT,\n468\t                    diff_summary TEXT,\n469\t                    diff_data    TEXT,\n470\t                    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n471\t                    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n472\t                    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n473\t                    FOREIGN KEY (previous_result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n474\t                )\n475\t            \"\"\")\n476\t\n477\t            # Copy data from old table to new table - explicit column mapping\n478\t            # to handle different column orders between old and new schemas\n479\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n480\t                INSERT INTO results_new (id, intent_id, topic_id, session_id, summary, data, urgency, result_type, card_fallback, created_at, surfaced_at, acked_at, previous_result_id, diff_summary, diff_data)\n481\t                SELECT id, intent_id, topic_id, session_id, summary, data, urgency,\n482\t                       COALESCE(result_type, 'default') AS result_type,\n483\t                       COALESCE(card_fallback, 0) AS card_fallback,\n484\t                       created_at, surfaced_at, acked_at, previous_result_id, diff_summary, diff_data\n485\t                FROM results\n486\t            \"\"\")\n487\t\n488\t            # Recreate indexes\n489\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_session\")\n490\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_topic\")\n491\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_created\")\n492\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_previous\")\n493\t\n494\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_session ON results_new(session_id)\")\n495\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_topic ON results_new(topic_id)\")\n496\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_created ON results_new(created_at)\")\n497\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_previous ON results_new(previous_result_id)\")\n498\t\n499\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n500\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE results\")\n501\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results_new RENAME TO results\")\n502\t\n503\t            await db.commit()\n504\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: results.intent_id now allows NULL\")\n505\t        except Exception as e:\n506\t            await db.rollback()\n507\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate results.intent_id: {e}\")\n508\t            raise\n509\t\n510\t    @staticmethod\n511\t    async def _migrate_bead_watch_comment_high_water(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n512\t        \"\"\"Migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1.\n513\t\n514\t        The initial schema had DEFAULT 0, which meant the first comment (index 0)\n515\t        was skipped on the first check. With DEFAULT -1, no comments are skipped\n516\t        initially, and only comments with index > comment_high_water are processed.\n517\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN DEFAULT, so we recreate the table.\n518\t        \"\"\"\n519\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the bead_watch table schema\n520\t        async with db.execute(\n521\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='bead_watch'\"\n522\t        ) as cur:\n523\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n524\t            if not table_sql:\n525\t                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n526\t\n527\t            # If comment_high_water already has DEFAULT -1, skip migration\n528\t            if \"DEFAULT -1\" in table_sql[0]:\n529\t                # Already migrated\n530\t                return\n531\t\n532\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new default\n533\t        logger.info(\"Migrating bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1\")\n534\t\n535\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n536\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n537\t\n538\t        try:\n539\t            # Create new table with DEFAULT -1\n540\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n541\t                CREATE TABLE bead_watch_new (\n542\t                    bead_ref           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n543\t                    refusal_count      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n544\t                    last_refusal_reason TEXT,\n545\t                    last_refusal_at    INTEGER,\n546\t                    comment_high_water INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT -1,\n547\t                    sla_deadline       INTEGER NOT NULL,\n548\t                    sla_flagged_at     INTEGER,\n549\t                    fenced_at          INTEGER,\n550\t                    created_at         INTEGER NOT NULL\n551\t                )\n552\t            \"\"\")\n553\t\n554\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n555\t            # Existing rows with comment_high_water=0 are migrated as-is\n556\t            # (they represent \"comment 0 has been processed\", which is valid)\n557\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n558\t                INSERT INTO bead_watch_new\n559\t                SELECT * FROM bead_watch\n560\t            \"\"\")\n561\t\n562\t            # Recreate indexes\n563\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline\")\n564\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_bead_watch_fenced\")\n565\t\n566\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline ON bead_watch_new(sla_deadline)\")\n567\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_bead_watch_fenced ON bead_watch_new(fenced_at)\")\n568\t\n569\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n570\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE bead_watch\")\n571\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE bead_watch_new RENAME TO bead_watch\")\n572\t\n573\t            await db.commit()\n574\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: bead_watch.comment_high_water default is now -1\")\n575\t        except Exception as e:\n576\t            await db.rollback()\n577\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water: {e}\")\n578\t            raise\n579\t\n580\t    async def close(self) -> None:\n581\t        \"\"\"Close database connection pool.\"\"\"\n582\t        # aiosqlite uses connection-per-context, so just ensure checkpoint\n583\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n584\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)\")\n585\t\n586\t    # one transaction.\n637\t\n638\t        The topics table references sessions with ``ON DELETE SET NULL``, so\n639\t        topics are *not* cascade-removed even if FKs were on — deleting them\n640\t Ensure the row exists (created_at set once, on first write).\n1335\t            await db.execute(\n1336\t                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO dispatch_timings (intent_id, created_at) VALUES (?, ?)\",\n1337\t                (intent_id, now),\n1338\t            )\n1339\t            if cols:\n1340\t                set_clause = \", \".join(f\"{c} = ?\" for c in cols)\n1341\t                params = [*cols.values(), intent_id]\n1342\t                await db.execute(\n1343\t                    f\"UPDATE dispatch_timings SET {set_clause} WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n1344\t                    params,\n1345\t                )\n1346\t            await db.commit()\n1347\t\n1348\t    async def get_dispatch_timings(self, intent_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n1349\t        \"\"\"Get the dispatch_timings row for one intent thread, or None.\"\"\"\n1350\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1351\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n1352\t            async with db.execute(\n1353\t                \"SELECT * FROM dispatch_timings WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n1354\t                (intent_id,),\n1355\t            ) as cursor:\n1356\t                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n1357\t                return dict(row) if row else None\n1358\t\n1359\t    async def get_latency_percentiles(self, since: int | None = None) -> dict:\n1360\t        \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per stage across dispatch_timings rows.\n1361\t\n1362\t        Returns ``{stage: {\"p50\": ms, \"p95\": ms, \"count\": n}}`` for every stage\n1363\t        that has at least one non-NULL value. NULLs are skipped per stage (so a\n1364\t        stage not yet captured for any dispatch simply doesn't appear). Pass\n1365\t        ``since`` (a unix timestamp) to window to recent dispatches only — the\n1366\t        latency-baseline bead consumes the un-windowed call to fill the plan's\n1367\t        global Measured p50/p95 columns.\n1368\t\n1369\t        Percentiles are nearest-rank (ceil(q/100 * n)), computed in Python\n1370\t        because SQLite has no built-in percentile.\n1371\t        \"\"\"\n1372\t        from ..instrument.timings import percentiles\n1373\t\n1374\t        result: dict[str, dict] = {}\n1375\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1376\t            for stage in DISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS:\n1377\t                if since is not None:\n1378\t                    query = (\n1379\t                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n1380\t                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= ? \"\n1381\t                        f\"ORDER BY {stage}\"\n1382\t                    )\n1383\t                    params: tuple = (since,)\n1384\t                else:\n1385\t                    query = (\n1386\t                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n1387\t                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL ORDER BY {stage}\"\n1388\t                    )\n1389\t                    params = ()\n1390\t                async with db.execute(query, params) as cursor:\n1391\t                    values = [row[0] for row in await cursor.fetchall()]\n1392\t                if not values:\n1393\t                    continue\n1394\t                pct = percentiles(values, qs=(50, 95))\n1395\t                result[stage] = {\n1396\t                    \"p50\": pct[50],\n1397\t                    \"p95\": pct[95],\n1398\t                    \"count\": len(values),\n1399\t                }\n1400\t        return result\n1401\t\n1402\t    async def get_session_feedback_summary(\n1403\t        self,\n1404\t        session_id: str,\n1405\t        since: int | None = None,\n1406\t    ) -> dict:\n1407\t        \"\"\"Get a summary of feedback signals for a session.\"\"\"\n1408\t        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n1409\t\n1410\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1411\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n1412\t\n1413\t            # Get signal counts by type\n1414\t            since_clause = since or one_hour_ago\n1415\t            async with db.execute(\n1416\t                \"\"\"SELECT signal_type, COUNT(*) as count\n1417\t                   FROM feedback_signals\n1418\t                   WHERE session_id = ?     )\n1473\t\n1474\t            if diff_result.has_changes:\n1475\t                has_diff = True\n1476\t                diff_summary = diff_result.change_summary\n1477\t                diff_data =       for f in diff_result.fields\n1486\t                    ],\n1487\t                    \"summary\": diff_result.summary,\n1488\t                }\n1489\t\n1490\t        # Create the result with
